api/Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data.DbC.title: Class DbCommandLoggerExtensions | Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data Documentation
api/Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data.DbC.summary: Class DbCommandLoggerExtensions Namespace Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data Assembly Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data.dll Extension methods on ILogger for logging a SQL command's text alongside its parameters, with opt-in redaction of sensitive parameter values. public static class DbCommandLoggerExtensions Inheritance object DbCommandLoggerExtensions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Two parameter-supplying styles are provided: An explicit IReadOnlyDictionary<TKey, TValue> of parameter name to value — the lowest-overhead path. A Dapper-style anonymous object (e.g. new { id = 1, name = "abc" }) — its public readable properties are reflected into a dictionary and then logged via the dictionary path. Per-type property accessors are cached so the reflection cost is paid once per parameter type, not once per call. Parameter-name matching for excludedParameterNames is case-insensitive and prefix-tolerant: @name, :name, ?name, and name are all treated as the same parameter, so a connection's ADO.NET prefix convention does not have to be known by the caller. The value of an excluded parameter is replaced with *** in the logged output; the parameter name itself is preserved so the shape of the command is still visible. Methods LogCommandText(ILogger, string, IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object?>) Logs the commandText and its parameters at LogLevel.Information. public static void LogCommandText(this ILogger logger, string commandText, IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object?> parameters) Parameters logger ILogger The logger to write to. commandText string The SQL command text. parameters IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object> The command parameters, keyed by name. Examples var p = new Dictionary<string, object?> { ["id"] = 1, ["password"] = "hunter2" }; logger.LogCommandText("SELECT * FROM Users WHERE Id = @id", p, new[] { "password" }); Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when logger, commandText, or parameters is null. LogCommandText(ILogger, string, IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object?>, LogLevel) Logs the commandText and its parameters at the specified level. public static void LogCommandText(this ILogger logger, string commandText, IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object?> parameters, LogLevel level) Parameters logger ILogger The logger to write to. commandText string The SQL command text. parameters IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object> The command parameters, keyed by name. level LogLevel The log level to write the entry at. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when logger, commandText, or parameters is null. LogCommandText(ILogger, string, IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object?>, IEnumerable<string>) Logs the commandText and its parameters at LogLevel.Information, redacting the values of any parameters named in excludedParameterNames. public static void LogCommandText(this ILogger logger, string commandText, IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object?> parameters, IEnumerable<string> excludedParameterNames) Parameters logger ILogger The logger to write to. commandText string The SQL command text. parameters IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object> The command parameters, keyed by name. excludedParameterNames IEnumerable<string> Parameter names whose values should be redacted. Matching is case-insensitive and prefix-tolerant (@name, :name, ?name, name are equivalent). Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when logger, commandText, parameters, or excludedParameterNames is null. LogCommandText(ILogger, string, IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object?>, IEnumerable<string>, LogLevel) Logs the commandText and its parameters at the specified level, redacting the values of any parameters named in excludedParameterNames. public static void LogCommandText(this ILogger logger, string commandText, IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object?> parameters, IEnumerable<string> excludedParameterNames, LogLevel level) Parameters logger ILogger The logger to write to. commandText string The SQL command text. parameters IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object> The command parameters, keyed by name. excludedParameterNames IEnumerable<string> Parameter names whose values should be redacted. Matching is case-insensitive and prefix-tolerant (@name, :name, ?name, name are equivalent). level LogLevel The log level to write the entry at. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when logger, commandText, parameters, or excludedParameterNames is null. LogCommandText(ILogger, string, object) Logs the commandText and the public readable properties of the anonymous (or any) parameters object at LogLevel.Information. public static void LogCommandText(this ILogger logger, string commandText, object parameters) Parameters logger ILogger The logger to write to. commandText string The SQL command text. parameters object An object whose public readable properties become the command parameters — e.g. new { id = 1, name = "abc" }. Examples logger.LogCommandText( "SELECT * FROM Users WHERE Id = @id", new { id = 1, password = "hunter2" }, new[] { "password" }); Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when logger, commandText, or parameters is null. LogCommandText(ILogger, string, object, LogLevel) Logs the commandText and the public readable properties of the parameters object at the specified level. public static void LogCommandText(this ILogger logger, string commandText, object parameters, LogLevel level) Parameters logger ILogger The logger to write to. commandText string The SQL command text. parameters object An object whose public readable properties become the command parameters. level LogLevel The log level to write the entry at. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when logger, commandText, or parameters is null. LogCommandText(ILogger, string, object, IEnumerable<string>) Logs the commandText and the public readable properties of the parameters object at LogLevel.Information, redacting the values of any properties named in excludedParameterNames. public static void LogCommandText(this ILogger logger, string commandText, object parameters, IEnumerable<string> excludedParameterNames) Parameters logger ILogger The logger to write to. commandText string The SQL command text. parameters object An object whose public readable properties become the command parameters. excludedParameterNames IEnumerable<string> Parameter names whose values should be redacted. Matching is case-insensitive and prefix-tolerant (@name, :name, ?name, name are equivalent). Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when logger, commandText, parameters, or excludedParameterNames is null. LogCommandText(ILogger, string, object, IEnumerable<string>, LogLevel) Logs the commandText and the public readable properties of the parameters object at the specified level, redacting the values of any properties named in excludedParameterNames. public static void LogCommandText(this ILogger logger, string commandText, object parameters, IEnumerable<string> excludedParameterNames, LogLevel level) Parameters logger ILogger The logger to write to. commandText string The SQL command text. parameters object An object whose public readable properties become the command parameters. excludedParameterNames IEnumerable<string> Parameter names whose values should be redacted. Matching is case-insensitive and prefix-tolerant (@name, :name, ?name, name are equivalent). level LogLevel The log level to write the entry at. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when logger, commandText, parameters, or excludedParameterNames is null. LogDbCommand(ILogger, DbCommand) Logs a live DbCommand — its CommandText and the name/value pairs of its Parameters — at LogLevel.Information. public static void LogDbCommand(this ILogger logger, DbCommand command) Parameters logger ILogger The logger to write to. command DbCommand The command to log. Examples using var command = connection.CreateCommand(); command.CommandText = "SELECT * FROM Users WHERE Id = @id"; logger.LogDbCommand(command); Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when logger or command is null. LogDbCommand(ILogger, DbCommand, LogLevel) Logs a live DbCommand at the specified level. public static void LogDbCommand(this ILogger logger, DbCommand command, LogLevel level) Parameters logger ILogger The logger to write to. command DbCommand The command to log. level LogLevel The log level to write the entry at. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when logger or command is null. LogDbCommand(ILogger, DbCommand, IEnumerable<string>) Logs a live DbCommand at LogLevel.Information, redacting the values of any parameters named in excludedParameterNames. public static void LogDbCommand(this ILogger logger, DbCommand command, IEnumerable<string> excludedParameterNames) Parameters logger ILogger The logger to write to. command DbCommand The command to log. excludedParameterNames IEnumerable<string> Parameter names whose values should be redacted. Matching is case-insensitive and prefix-tolerant (@name, :name, ?name, name are equivalent). Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when logger, command, or excludedParameterNames is null. LogDbCommand(ILogger, DbCommand, IEnumerable<string>, LogLevel) Logs a live DbCommand at the specified level, redacting the values of any parameters named in excludedParameterNames. public static void LogDbCommand(this ILogger logger, DbCommand command, IEnumerable<string> excludedParameterNames, LogLevel level) Parameters logger ILogger The logger to write to. command DbCommand The command to log. excludedParameterNames IEnumerable<string> Parameter names whose values should be redacted. Matching is case-insensitive and prefix-tolerant (@name, :name, ?name, name are equivalent). level LogLevel The log level to write the entry at. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when logger, command, or excludedParameterNames is null.

api/Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data.DbC.title: Class DbConnectionLoggerExtensions | Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data Documentation
api/Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data.DbC.summary: Class DbConnectionLoggerExtensions Namespace Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data Assembly Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data.dll Extension methods on ILogger for logging DbConnection instances. public static class DbConnectionLoggerExtensions Inheritance object DbConnectionLoggerExtensions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks The ConnectionString is parsed via DbConnectionStringBuilder and the Password and Pwd keys are removed before logging so credentials are never written to the log. Key matching is case-insensitive per the DbConnectionStringBuilder contract, so password, PASSWORD, etc. are all redacted. All other keys, including the user name (User ID, UID, etc.), are preserved. Methods LogDbConnection(ILogger, DbConnection) Logs a single structured entry describing the connection at LogLevel.Information. public static void LogDbConnection(this ILogger logger, DbConnection connection) Parameters logger ILogger The logger to write to. connection DbConnection The connection to log. Examples using var connection = new SqlConnection(connectionString); await connection.OpenAsync(); logger.LogDbConnection(connection); Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when logger or connection is null. LogDbConnection(ILogger, DbConnection, LogLevel) Logs a single structured entry describing the connection at the specified level. public static void LogDbConnection(this ILogger logger, DbConnection connection, LogLevel level) Parameters logger ILogger The logger to write to. connection DbConnection The connection to log. level LogLevel The log level to write the entry at. Examples using var connection = new SqlConnection(connectionString); await connection.OpenAsync(); logger.LogDbConnection(connection, LogLevel.Debug); Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when logger or connection is null.

api/Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data.Ent.title: Class EntityFramework6Logging | Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data Documentation
api/Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data.Ent.summary: Class EntityFramework6Logging Namespace Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data.EntityFramework6 Assembly Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data.EntityFramework6.dll One-call registration of passive EF6 logging. Calling AddLoggingInterceptors once at startup wires command, connection, and transaction interceptors into the process-global EF6 interception pipeline, so every DbContext in the process logs its activity with no per-query call sites. public static class EntityFramework6Logging Inheritance object EntityFramework6Logging Inherited Members object.ToString() object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() Remarks Add(IDbInterceptor) is process-global and not idempotent — adding the same interceptor twice doubles every log entry. To make the common "call it once per logger" pattern safe, this type keeps a static record of the loggers it has already registered and treats a repeat registration of the same logger instance as a no-op. Methods AddLoggingInterceptors(ILogger, IEnumerable<string>?, LogLevel) Registers command, connection, and transaction logging interceptors for the given logger into the EF6 interception pipeline. Safe to call more than once with the same logger instance — repeat calls are a no-op. public static void AddLoggingInterceptors(ILogger logger, IEnumerable<string>? excludedParameterNames = null, LogLevel level = LogLevel.Information) Parameters logger ILogger The logger every EF6 event is written to. excludedParameterNames IEnumerable<string> Optional parameter names whose values are redacted in logged commands. Matching is case-insensitive and ADO.NET-prefix-tolerant. When null, nothing is redacted. level LogLevel The level every non-error event is logged at. Defaults to LogLevel.Information. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when logger is null.

api/Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data.Ent.title: Namespace Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data.EntityFramework6 | Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data Documentation
api/Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data.Ent.summary: Namespace Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data.EntityFramework6 Classes EntityFramework6Logging One-call registration of passive EF6 logging. Calling AddLoggingInterceptors once at startup wires command, connection, and transaction interceptors into the process-global EF6 interception pipeline, so every DbContext in the process logs its activity with no per-query call sites.
